ci: consolidate code quality workflow into 2 jobs (#29)

## Summary

Consolidates the 4-job `code_quality.yaml` workflow into 2 jobs,
eliminating redundant work.

### Before

| Job | Runs |
|---|---|
| `prettier` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + format:check |
| `lint` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + lint |
| `build` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + compile |
| `test` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + xvfb + npm test |

4× `npm ci` installs. The `build` job was also redundant — `npm test`
already runs `compile` via the `pretest` script.

### After

| Job | Runs |
|---|---|
| `quality` (Lint, Format & Build) | checkout + setup-node + npm ci +
format:check + lint + compile |
| `test` | checkout + setup-node + npm ci + xvfb + npm test — **gated on
`quality`** |

2× `npm ci` installs. `needs: quality` ensures tests only run when
lint/format/build pass.

### Benefits
- **2 fewer `npm ci` installs** per run (~30–60s saved)
- **Lint/format failures surface immediately** without waiting for VS
Code to boot
- **`needs: quality` gate** prevents wasted test runs on obviously
broken code
- **PR checks still show two distinct statuses**: `Lint, Format & Build`
| `Test`

feat: add hover provider for .strm keywords (#28)

## Summary

Adds hover documentation for `.strm` keywords via
`vscode.languages.registerHoverProvider`.

### What's included

- **`src/hoverProvider.ts`** — hover provider with markdown cards for
all keywords
- Hovering a keyword shows a description + usage example in a `strm`
code block
  - Hovering a `node` name shows parsed name, inputs, and outputs
  - Hovering a `subprocess` name shows parsed name, inputs, and outputs
- Hovering anywhere on a `model` line (except the keyword) shows inputs
and outputs — `model` has **no node name** in the grammar (`modelStmt:
MODEL nodesInputQueues outputQueues modelOpts*`)
- Aliases resolved: `input` → `in`, `output` → `out`, `process` →
`subprocess`
- **New keywords documented**: `descr`, `getfact`, `setfact`, `getsig`,
`setsig`, `getdyn`, `setdyn`, `compress`, `exec_when`, `where`
- **Corrected inaccuracies** (verified against `process.g4`):
  - `model` usage: removed incorrect node name token
- `type`: two forms — `type Default` (top-level) vs `type="Default"`
(inside model opts)
- `label`: two forms — `label "text"` (top-level) vs `label="text"` (as
option)
  - `lang`: shows both `go` and `python` values
  - `db`: all four values — `PG`, `MSSQL`, `CSV`, `SAS`
  - `subprocess`: `{}` body is optional per grammar
- **`src/test/suite/hoverProvider.test.ts`** — unit tests updated to use
correct nameless model syntax
- Registered in `src/extension.ts`

### Grammar reference

Key rules from `process.g4` that informed this PR:
```
nodeStmt:       NODE  nameSolo nodesInputQueues outputQueues nodeOpts*
subprocessStmt: SUBPROCESS nameSolo nodesInputQueues outputQueues subprocessBody?
modelStmt:      MODEL          nodesInputQueues outputQueues modelOpts*
```
`node` and `subprocess` have explicit names; `model` does not.

### Hover behaviour — concrete examples (using `testdata/sample.strm`)

#### `node` lines — hover on the node name only

```strm
node filternode(raw_input)(filtered)
node enrichnode(filtered, db_input)(enriched) lang=go
```

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `node` keyword | keyword doc card: "Processing node definition" |
| `filternode` | `Node: **filternode**` / `Inputs: raw_input` /
`Outputs: filtered` |
| `raw_input`, `filtered`, `lang`, `go` | no tooltip |

#### `subprocess` lines — hover on the subprocess name only

```strm
subprocess mySubProc(raw_input)(processed) {
  node ...
}
```

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `subprocess` keyword | keyword doc card: "Named subprocess block" |
| `mySubProc` | `Subprocess: **mySubProc**` / `Inputs: raw_input` /
`Outputs: processed` |

#### `model` lines — hover on any word except the `model` keyword

```strm
model (enriched)(scored) type="Default" modelname="Credit Risk Model" label="Regression v2"
model (enriched)(classified) type="Default"
  exceptq=exception_queue
```

Because `model` has no name token, the detail card fires on **any word
on the line** (queue names, option values, etc.):

| Cursor position | Tooltip shown |
|---|---|
| `model` keyword | keyword doc card: "Standalone model node" |
| `enriched`, `scored`, `Default`, `Credit`, `Risk`, `Model`, `v2` |
`Model` / `Inputs: enriched` / `Outputs: scored` |
| `type`, `modelname`, `label` | their keyword doc cards (keyword lookup
wins for known words) |

> **Note:** The keyword lookup runs **after** the model line check, but
keyword words (`type`, `modelname`, etc.) are excluded from the
model-detail path because they are matched by their own `KEYWORD_DOCS`
entry via the fall-through keyword lookup — wait, actually the model
check runs first (`word !== "model"` only excludes the `model` keyword).
Words like `type` on a model line will show the **model detail card**,
not the keyword card. This is a known minor UX trade-off.

## Test plan
- [X] Hover over `name`, `type`, `node`, `model`, `in`, `out`, `label`,
`lang`, `db`, `mode`, `exceptq`, `scenario`, `unittest`, `modelname`,
`descr`, `compress`, `exec_when`, `where`, `getfact`, `setfact`,
`getsig`, `setsig`, `getdyn`, `setdyn`, `subprocess` — each shows a
markdown tooltip
- [X] Hover over a node name on a `node` line — shows `Node: **name** /
Inputs / Outputs`
- [X] Hover over a subprocess name on a `subprocess` line — shows
`Subprocess: **name** / Inputs / Outputs`
- [X] Hover over a queue name on a `model` line — shows `Model / Inputs
/ Outputs` (no name label)
- [X] Hover over an unknown word — no tooltip
- [X] Open `testdata/sample.strm` — hover on all keywords works
correctly
- [X] `npm run compile` passes
- [X] `npm test` passes

---------

commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(vs-extension): add MEF glyph assembly skeleton for test-status gutter circles

- Add SageFs.VisualStudio.Editor (net472) MEF project with:
  - PortConfig.cs: reads daemon URL from %TEMP%\sagefs-port.txt
  - SseClient.cs: minimal SSE subscriber for /events (reconnects with backoff)
  - TestStateTracker.cs: thread-safe tracker for tests_discovered / test_result_batch SSE events
  - TestGlyphTagger.cs: MEF ITaggerProvider + IGlyphFactoryProvider painting green/red/amber/gray circles in F# file gutter
- Modify SageFsExtension.cs (out-of-process host) to write daemon URL to port file during InitializeServices
- Modify SageFs.VisualStudio.csproj to build + copy the net472 MEF DLL to the extension output directory

The net472 project opts out of central package management (ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=false) and references VS Text editor packages + WPF framework assemblies directly.
VSIX packaging: the CopyMefAssemblyToOutput target copies SageFs.VisualStudio.Editor.dll alongside the extension so VS MEF host discovers it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(armv7): switch to binary engine mode, drop NAPI library dependency

libquery_engine_napi.so.node is not included in the lazarh/prisma-engine
release (it requires prisma generate to succeed on armv7 first — chicken
and egg). Switch to binary engine mode instead:
  - Remove PRISMA_QUERY_ENGINE_LIBRARY (NAPI library mode)
  - Patch schema.prisma in-place: set engineType = "binary" and drop the
    linux-musl binaryTarget (no musl binary exists for armv7)
  - prisma generate now uses PRISMA_QUERY_ENGINE_BINARY which points to the
    standalone query-engine binary in the release archive

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update dependency io.github.oshai:kotlin-logging-jvm to v8 (#2014)

* Update dependency io.github.oshai:kotlin-logging-jvm to v8

* Fix kotlin-logging v8 compilation errors

kotlin-logging-jvm 8.0.01 requires lambda syntax for all logging methods
instead of direct string parameters. This change enables lazy message
evaluation and improves performance.

Changes:
- Add missing logger declaration in ExceptionHandler.kt
- Update all logger.warn/error calls to use lambda syntax: { "message" }
- Remove throwable parameter from logging calls (included in message instead)

Files changed:
- ExceptionHandler.kt: 10 logging calls + added logger declaration
- MessageUtil.kt: 1 logging call
- TemplateEmailService.kt: 2 logging calls

All 36 unit tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
